verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted but exists in a large latent complex (LLC) where the mature peptide homodimer is non-covalently/covalently tethered to LAP and LTBP. LTBP proteins are anchored to the extracellular matrix and to cell-surface proteins (e.g., GARP/LRRC32, LRRC33) via disulfide and wash-resistant interactions, creating a stable cell-surface-tethered pool on regulatory T cells, platelets, and myeloid cells. This constitutes a documented stable surface attachment via a TM scaffold partner (GARP), qualifying as wash-resistant anchoring rather than freely secreted equilibrium. The surface-tethered latent complex is a validated therapeutic target for surface-directed antibodies.
